canonFacts.statement: Elias is a dwarf who serves as quartermaster for Iconoclasm, working in the storeroom sorting spears.
Illmari Vaino is the founder of Iconoclasm.
Gin Soaked Rag is associated with the Sin and Tonic.
Anaïs and Anouk are adventurers of approximately level 11.
Dura Oil Drinker is a mechanic.
The Roundhat Gang is run by Tywelwyn Leatherhide.
Tywelwyn Leatherhide ordered material from Embercall Industries, which is associated with the Pyrelight faction.
Pyrelight is a faction whose associated company name is Embercall Industries.
The law enforcement authority in Sableclutch is called the Threshold Authority, also known as the TA.
Flynn was found dead in a spooky underground area filled with bones, apparently having been dead for some time before the party arrived.
Augers are creatures created through a divination ritual; they are an offshoot of that ritual and can be fed to improve divination.
Iconoclasm has a company vehicle (referred to as a company car) available for use by its members.

A conspiracy theorist known as Whiskers told the party about an entity or phenomenon called the Zorbon, claiming it is connected to mushrooms and radio signals, and that tinfoil hats provide protection.
The Sin and Tonic is described as roughly a two-star establishment — not luxurious, but not the worst either.
The Sin and Tonic is a single-story building; it has no second floor, with sleeping quarters at the back of the pub.

A Fane is a space whose existence and size are sustained by a magical object called a Heart; the size of a Fane is directly related to the amount of magical energy available to the Heart maintaining it.
Multiple Hearts can jointly maintain a single Fane, a technique used by large organizations to sustain large-footprint headquarters.
Belvedere has integrated Hearts into its civic hierarchy more than most places, and Fanes are especially numerous there.

Concordia is a major corporation in this world, equivalent to consumer-electronics giants like LG or Samsung, producing appliances and home-facing goods. They standardize the branding of their consumer-facing Hearts so that all Fanes they maintain appear consistent.

The RunGunners are a particularly famous group of mercenaries and guns-for-hire who maintain an entire city in the middle of a desert, sustained as one very large Fane powered by many Hearts of different personalities, resulting in a clashing, mishmash environment.
When Hearts of different origins jointly maintain a Fane, the areas where their influences meet appear strange and patchwork.
The Heart of the Sin and Tonic is named Ginny (also called 'Gin Soaked Rag'). Ginny's physical form is a suit vest and dress pants, with a twisted-up dish rag in place of a head.
Ginny tends bar at the Sin and Tonic and can cook food, though he is less skilled at cooking human food than Illmari.
The Scale empowers its arbiters to conduct bounty hunting; the Scale does not hire random individuals for such work, as arbiters must be formally empowered by the Scale to act in that capacity.
Three police organizations operate in Hallia and post bounties for outstanding warrants that independent bounty hunters can pursue.
In Hallia, particularly strong magic set up by the Host prevents people from sliding between city districts freely; residents must use public transit to travel between districts.
Hallia has a trolley/tram system that runs along a route called 'the Horizon', connecting different districts of the city.
The Undertable is a pawn shop located in Sableclutch, Star 12 in Hallia, situated starward down the Horizon from the Sin and Tonic neighborhood.
The district around the Sin and Tonic is a commercialized, generally poorer area of Hallia where people earn enough to get by but are not well-to-do.

Further starward in Hallia, buildings become older and less well-maintained; they were built when a tram line was first constructed in anticipation of a boom that never materialized, and have been neglected for decades.
Anzu has a raven named Othello who followed him from Belvedere to Hallia and rejoined the party outside the Sin and Tonic.
Anzu is a member of the Belvedere faction; his family has multiple members involved in Belvedere across different areas.
Anzu's father is an archivist within Belvedere.
Anzu's mother is not a member of Belvedere; she manages the family's affairs, as the family is relatively well-off.
The Undertable is a narrow, windowless shop squeezed between two former homes, identified by a handwritten sign reading 'Under Table' with a crude drawing of a table.

The Undertable's interior is crammed with a wide variety of goods including kitchen appliances, loose jewelry, eight different radios in various states of deconstruction, and a large unlicensed collection of firearms.
Smiler is the proprietor of the Undertable and also operates as a fence; he is a centaur standing approximately nine feet tall.
Johnny has a prior working relationship with Smiler and has dealt with him at the Undertable on at least a couple of occasions before the current session.

Smiler is heavily adorned with jewelry: multiple yellowish metal bands on both arms, gems braided into his tail, flare gauges in his ears with gems embedded in them, and a long beard braided in the same style as his tail with gems throughout.
Johnny is currently affiliated with Iconoclasm and has been lent over to them, a fact he discloses to Smiler.
Smiler extends his friends discount to Johnny's companions on the basis that a friend of Johnny's would not inform on him to the authorities.
Smiler is a pawn shop owner who buys and sells goods, including radios and equipment such as a poly tool priced at nine gold pieces.
Benny is nine years old and came from Belvedere, called there by the Voidsong as the other party members were.
Johnny is half-Elven.
Argyle is approximately 20 years old in human years.
Benny's father is named Atum, who is associated with Belvedere and may be an archivist.
Belvedere functions as a school and place of education, among other things, and children there receive an education.
Children from Belvedere do not normally receive permission to travel hundreds of miles into the field; Benny's case is considered a special exception.
Johnny is originally from Raelion, which has since been destroyed.
Anzu and Benny are from Austrene.
Johnny lived in a small mountain town called Helmstier, located right on the border between two countries.
Johnny ran a tavern called The Last Stop in Helmstier, named for being the last stop before leaving or entering either bordering country.
Argyle's parents are operatives of the Scale.
Argyle's parents are named Aaron Cross and Joran Davinrock; they have different last names.

Sableclutch is a neighborhood in Hallia, located somewhat near the city center but on the voidward horizon, and contains a number of religious areas including several churches, mostly dedicated to the Host but also to a smattering of other gods.

Sableclutch Scrap is a scrapyard located in the Sableclutch neighborhood of Hallia, situated voidward and down the horizon from the city center, in an area near factories that dump waste into the river running through Hallia.
Hallia has a public tram system that the party uses to travel between neighborhoods.
The voidward part of Hallia is mostly factories that dump waste into the river running through the city, with little residential population in that direction.
Copperjaw was referred to the party by Anouk (also called Onnick), who told them he always needs people for jobs.

Copperjaw is a dwarven man who operates Sableclutch Scrap; he has a mashed-up, misshapen nose, a black eye, and his lower jaw has been replaced with a crude copper prosthetic, which is the origin of his name.
Copperjaw appears to be near the end of middle age or beginning of elderly by dwarven standards, which corresponds to roughly 120–130 years old.
Anzu's animal companion Othello is a bird that follows the party in the sky at roughly 100 feet altitude and can communicate with Anzu empathically (without words).
Threshold Authority is a law-enforcement organization in the city, identifiable by greenish uniforms bearing the letters 'TA'.
Threshold Authority is a law enforcement organization whose officers wear greenish uniforms marked with 'TA'.
Black Line is a separate law-enforcement organization that generally polices the nicer parts of the city; its officers wear full uniforms and carry batons rather than firearms.
Blackline is a private law enforcement company that generally polices nicer parts of the city.
Blackline officers carry batons as their primary weapon rather than firearms.
Blackline operates on a subscription model, providing law enforcement services only to paying clients.
Copperjaw operates out of a scrapyard called Sableclutch Scrap and is known to hire people to make deliveries.
Private vehicles with built-in slide devices can travel between city districts (voidward or starward), but slides cannot be used within city limits as a security measure.
A standalone slide device that allows travel anywhere outside city limits costs approximately two gold pieces.
The Masque District contains hotels and is a tourist area of the city.
Tourists staying in the city lodge in an area called the Masque District.
The tram system uses numbered stations; the party boarded near the factory/scrapyard district at Void 2 and their destination is at Prime (Zero).
The tram line runs through stations designated by 'Void' numbers, with the party boarding at Void 2 and their destination being at Prime (Zero).
The tram system in the city is privately operated by Prime Meridian.
A taxi fare in the city costs approximately three silver pieces.
The Ugathals have a featureless face from which a proboscis can grow to drain blood from a target.
The Ugathals have a blood nourishment ability that is a three-action manipulate action; when successfully used on a grabbed target, it grants the Ugathal benefits (described as nourishment).
The campaign setting is called Færrin.
